items.q: What is the function of synovial fluid in a joint?
items.correct: It lubricates the joint, reducing friction during movement.
items.random_wrong: It hardens to protect the bone.
items.why: Synovial fluid keeps freely movable joints smooth, cutting friction and wear.

items.q: Which of these belongs to the axial skeleton?
items.correct: The vertebral column (also the skull and ribs).
items.why: The axial skeleton is the central core - skull, spine, ribs - while limb bones are appendicular.

items.q: Does a ligament join muscle to bone?
items.correct: No - a ligament joins bone to bone; a tendon joins muscle to bone.
items.misconception: Yes, ligaments join muscle to bone.
items.random_wrong: A ligament is a type of bone.
items.why: Ligaments stabilise joints by linking bones; tendons transmit muscle force to bone.

items.q: How many bones are in the adult human skeleton?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 206. “106” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
It's Hip. “Elbow” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Number of bones in the adult human body?
items.why: Exact answer: 206 bones. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
